repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 5 Series 524td / 524d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Viable on a well-maintained E28/E34; rebuild or exchange
Only viable on low-mileage, otherwise scrap and replace
Simple, affordable repair — always worth doing
Replacement often £1,200–£2,200 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 5 Series 524td? Check These First
buyIf: * Well-maintained example with documented injection pump history
* Cylinder head checked — no coolant loss or overheating
* Service history with regular oil and fuel filter changes
* E34 model (improved electronics) if you want better parts availability
avoidIf: * Any hard starting or running issues — injection pump problems are expensive
* Evidence of coolant loss or overheating — cylinder head risk
* Poor service history — the M21 is forgiving but old
* Rust — classic BMWs are more valuable than the engine

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 524td is a piece of BMW history — the company's first 5 Series diesel. It's a simple, mechanical 2.4-litre six that's fundamentally robust but has aged. Injection pump wear and cylinder head cracking are the main concerns. Parts availability is becoming limited, making this a niche specialist buy. A well-maintained E34 524td is a charming, economical classic diesel.

cta.label: → Compare BMW 5 Series 524td engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 5 Series 524td / 524d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Any hard starting in cold weather? Check glow plugs
* Running Behaviour — Any juddering, hesitation, or black smoke? (injection pump warning)
* Service History — Oil and fuel filter changes are critical; check for regular maintenance
* Coolant Level — Any loss? Overheating? (cylinder head cracking risk)
* Turbo Response — Check boost; any lag or smoke? (turbo wear)
* Oil Consumption — Monitor between services; any excessive consumption?
* Engine Warning Light — E34: have codes been read? (simpler electronics)
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a BMW 5 Series 524td engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned BMW 5 Series 524td engine (M21D24) typically costs £1,200–£2,200 supply only. Fitting adds £900–£1,300. An injection pump rebuild costs £600–£1,200. Parts availability is becoming limited.

items.question: Is the BMW 5 Series 524td reliable?
items.answer: The M21 is fundamentally robust but aged. Injection pump wear is the most common failure, and cylinder head cracking can occur on high-mileage examples. The E34 version has improved electronics. Parts availability is becoming limited. A well-maintained example with a fresh injection pump can still cover high mileages.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW 5 Series 524td?
items.answer: Injection pump wear (hard starting, poor throttle, £600–£1,200), cylinder head cracking (coolant loss, overheating, £1,000–£2,500), and glow plug failure (hard starting in cold weather, £150–£400). The engine is otherwise simple and robust.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW 5 Series 524td engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: E28/E34 524td values range from £2,000–£8,000 depending on condition and mileage. A £1,200–£2,200 reconditioned engine represents 15–80% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement is often justified. For a higher-mileage example with other issues, assess scrap value first.

items.question: How do I find my BMW 5 Series 524td engine code?
items.answer: The 524td uses a single engine code: M21D24. This is BMW's first-generation 2.4-litre six-cylinder diesel. The 524d (rare in UK) uses the same block without turbo. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

items.question: Can I repair the injection pump issue, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — the injection pump can be rebuilt or exchanged (£600–£1,200). Symptoms include hard starting, poor throttle response, and juddering. Regular fuel filter changes help extend pump life. At the first sign of running issues, book an inspection — ignored pump problems can lead to injector and engine damage. A specialist diesel pump rebuilder is recommended.
